signal_title: 전기통신사업법 시행령 개정: 인앱결제 강제 금지
risk_rationale: The new regulation in South Korea prohibits forcing specific in-app payment methods, which directly impacts TikTok's current compliance baseline regarding payment processor independence and could result in significant fines if not addressed.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: No in-house payment processing in regulated markets
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prohibition of forced in-app payment methods
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The regulation prohibits forcing specific payment methods, which may conflict with existing practices if TikTok mandates certain payment processors.

remediations.title: Adjust Payment Method Policies in South Korea
remediations.description: Ensure that TikTok does not mandate specific payment methods for in-app purchases in South Korea. Update the payment processing policies to comply with the new regulation.
acceptance_criteria: No mandatory payment method enforced in South Korea

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new regulation could result in fines up to 2% of sales in South Korea, impacting revenue and market operations.
recommended_actions: Review and update payment processing policies in South Korea
Coordinate with legal team to ensure compliance with the new regulation
Communicate changes to relevant stakeholders and update user-facing policies
